footer_disclaimer: This site provides screening tools, not medical advice or diagnosis.
crisis_banner_title: If you are in crisis, help is available now
crisis_banner_text_line: Crisis Text Line: text HOME to 741741 (US and Canada) or 85258 (UK)
history_optin_label: Save my attempts to this browser
phq9_q1: Little interest or pleasure in doing things
phq9_q3: Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much
phq9_q4: Feeling tired or having little energy
phq9_q6: Feeling bad about yourself - or that you are a failure or have let yourself or your family down
phq9_q7: Trouble concentrating on things, such as reading the newspaper or watching television
phq9_q8: Moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed. Or the opposite - being so fidgety or restless that you have been moving around a lot more than usual
phq9_q9: Thoughts that you would be better off dead or of hurting yourself in some way
phq9_action_minimal: Minimal symptoms. No specific action recommended; continue monitoring if mood changes.
phq9_action_mild: Mild depressive symptoms. Watchful waiting; consider self-help resources; reassess in 2 weeks.
phq9_action_moderate: Moderate depression. Consider counselling, therapy, or talking with a primary care clinician.
phq9_action_mod_severe: Moderately severe depression. Active treatment with therapy and/or medication is typically warranted. Discuss with a clinician.
phq9_action_severe: Severe depression. Treatment is strongly indicated. Contact a clinician promptly. If you have thoughts of self-harm, reach out to a crisis line now.
phq2_short_desc: Patient Health Questionnaire-2 — depression quick screen
phq2_q1: Little interest or pleasure in doing things
phq2_action_negative: Score below 3. A negative result on this brief screen; if you remain concerned, the full PHQ-9 provides more detail.
phq2_action_positive: Score of 3 or higher. A positive screen for depression. Consider taking the full PHQ-9 for severity grading and discuss with a clinician.
gad7_q1: Feeling nervous, anxious or on edge
gad7_q2: Not being able to stop or control worrying
gad7_q3: Worrying too much about different things
gad7_q5: Being so restless that it is hard to sit still
gad7_q7: Feeling afraid as if something awful might happen
gad7_action_mild: Mild anxiety. Self-help and watchful waiting; reassess if symptoms persist.
gad7_action_moderate: Moderate anxiety. Consider further evaluation; therapy is often effective.
gad7_action_severe: Severe anxiety. Active treatment is typically indicated; discuss with a clinician.
cesd_short_desc: Center for Epidemiologic Studies Depression Scale
cesd_q1: I was bothered by things that usually don't bother me
cesd_q2: I did not feel like eating; my appetite was poor
cesd_q3: I felt that I could not shake off the blues even with help from my family or friends
cesd_q4: I felt I was just as good as other people
cesd_q5: I had trouble keeping my mind on what I was doing
cesd_q7: I felt that everything I did was an effort
cesd_q8: I felt hopeful about the future
cesd_q9: I thought my life had been a failure
cesd_q19: I felt that people disliked me
cesd_scale_0: Rarely or none of the time (less than 1 day)
cesd_scale_1: Some or a little of the time (1-2 days)
cesd_scale_2: Occasionally or a moderate amount of time (3-4 days)
cesd_scale_3: Most or all of the time (5-7 days)
cesd_action_negative: Score below 16. Below the conventional research cutoff for depressive symptoms.
cesd_action_positive: Score of 16 or higher. Above the conventional cutoff used in research for clinically significant depressive symptomatology. Consider clinical follow-up.
promis_depression_q7: I felt that I had nothing to look forward to
promis_depression_q8: I felt that nothing could cheer me up
promis_action_moderate: Moderate depressive symptoms. Consider professional evaluation.
promis_action_severe: Severe depressive symptoms. Professional support is indicated.
wsas_q1: Because of my problem, my ability to work is impaired
wsas_q2: Because of my problem, my home management (cleaning, tidying, shopping, cooking, looking after home or children, paying bills) is impaired
wsas_q3: Because of my problem, my social leisure activities (with other people, such as parties, bars, clubs, outings, visits, dating, home entertainment) are impaired
wsas_q4: Because of my problem, my private leisure activities (done alone, such as reading, gardening, collecting, sewing, walking alone) are impaired
wsas_q5: Because of my problem, my ability to form and maintain close relationships with others, including those I live with, is impaired
wsas_action_none: Score of 0. No functional impairment reported.
wsas_action_significant: Score 10-19. Significant functional impairment associated with the underlying problem.
wsas_action_severe: Score 20-40. Severe functional impairment; clinical attention warranted.
who5_q1: I have felt cheerful and in good spirits
who5_q2: I have felt calm and relaxed
who5_q3: I have felt active and vigorous
who5_q4: I woke up feeling fresh and rested
who5_q5: My daily life has been filled with things that interest me
who5_scale_2: Less than half of the time
who5_scale_3: More than half of the time
who5_action_very_low: Score 0-28. Very low wellbeing. Consider screening for depression with the PHQ-9 and discussing with a clinician.
who5_action_low: Score 29-50. Low wellbeing. Consider lifestyle changes; if persistent, consult a clinician.
who5_action_normal: Score 51-100. Wellbeing within the normal range.
